title: Magnetic Symmetries and the Structure of Correlation Functions in Quantum Field Theory
abstract: Quantum field theories in the presence of a static and uniform external magnetic field possess two characteristic spatial symmetries: magnetic translations and magnetic rotation. We investigate general consequences of these symmetries on correlation functions from a model-independent perspective, without relying on specific models or perturbative expansions. The projective structure of magnetic translation symmetry constrains correlation functions of charged operators to acquire the Schwinger phase and leads to a factorized form into a gauge-covariant phase factor and a reduced correlator depending only on relative coordinates. We further derive the spectral representation of two-point functions in terms of representations of the magnetic translation algebra, in which the Landau- and symmetric-gauge descriptions arise as different choices of basis. Our results provide a unified symmetry-based framework for quantum field theories in external magnetic fields.
summary: This theoretical paper develops a model-independent framework to analyze quantum field theories in strong magnetic fields by exploiting magnetic translation and rotation symmetries. It shows that these symmetries force correlation functions to factorize and acquire a Schwinger phase. Ultimately, the authors derive a rigorous spectral representation for these correlation functions, applicable to various physical systems in the presence of a magnetic field.
main_problem: To investigate the general consequences of magnetic symmetries (translations and rotations) on correlation functions in Quantum Field Theory when subjected to a static, uniform external magnetic field.
main_result: The magnetic translation symmetry forces correlation functions to acquire a Schwinger phase and leads to a factorized form, allowing for a model-independent spectral representation based on the magnetic translation algebra.
method: The analysis uses symmetry constraints derived from the projective structure of the magnetic translation algebra, avoiding specific models or perturbative expansions.
model_or_system: The system is a Quantum Field Theory (QFT) describing charged particles coupled to a background U(1) gauge field with a constant magnetic field (B) along the z-axis.
key_observables: Two-point and N-point Wightman/Green's functions, the Schwinger phase, and the spectral representation of the reduced correlator.
important_parameters: The magnetic field strength (B), the charge of the particles (q), and the transverse coordinates ($x_\perp$).
main_assumptions: The magnetic field is assumed to be static and uniform; the analysis assumes the magnetic symmetries are global symmetries acting on the dynamical fields.
paper_structure: The paper proceeds by first establishing the magnetic symmetries and their non-Abelian algebra (Chunk 2). It then applies these symmetries to constrain N-point correlation functions, deriving the Schwinger phase and factorization (Chunk 3). Finally, it derives the spectral representation of the reduced two-point function using the magnetic translation algebra, yielding basis-independent forms for both Landau and symmetric gauges (Chunks 4 & 5).
why_interesting: The derivation of spectral representations and correlation function factorizations based purely on symmetry principles provides a powerful, non-perturbative tool applicable to strongly correlated systems in external fields, which is highly relevant for understanding magnetotransport phenomena.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
